title: atomic-skills app-map catalog (schemaVersion 0.1)
description: Validates the persisted app-map.json information-architecture catalog emitted and consumed by the design-brief skill. The catalog records design-agnostic page facts, source provenance, and unresolved artefact/code conflicts so reconstruction can stop on ambiguity instead of guessing.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the persisted app-map contract. Constrained to the supported set (0.1 from F0, 0.2 from F1, 0.3 reshapes conflict into witnesses[]) so an unsupported version fails validation instead of false-greening; mirrors the enum convention of common.schema.json#/$defs/schemaVersion. Consumers branch on this before interpreting required fields. 0.2 (F1, Revisão 2) makes the per-page evidenceHash required and reforms conflict.resolution into an object recording the operator's arbitration; the frozen single-direction door applies from 0.2 onward. inputsHash stays as a top-level aggregate (a roll-up of the per-page evidenceHashes); the per-page evidenceHash is the granular staleness + conflict-suppression signal.

inputsHash.description: Content hash of the reconstruction source set. This is the staleness signal for deciding whether the catalog still matches its inputs.

projectId.description: Optional project identifier for catalogs generated from a multi-project source tree.

aliases.description: Optional advisory alternate catalog names or lookup keys. Aliases are advisory only and must not override canonical ids.

pages.description: Design-agnostic logical pages in the application information architecture.

allOf.description: 0.2+ (single-direction door): every page must carry a per-page evidenceHash. Applied conditionally so 0.1 catalogs (no per-page hash) stay valid; the base page.required is untouched. The door applies from 0.2 onward, so 0.3 inherits it.

0.1/0.2: conflicts use the two-slot legacy descriptor (artefactValue/codeValue). Gated by version so the 0.3 witnesses[] shape is not admitted here. The base page.conflicts.items stays permissive (type object) so these version branches own the shape without an additionalProperties contradiction across allOf.

0.3 (P1 — never choose in silence): a conflict is a SET of witnesses, not two positional slots. Each conflict carries witnesses[{value, source, kind}] with N>=1 (N=3 representable); the removed artefactValue/codeValue slots are rejected by conflict_0_3's additionalProperties:false.

page.description: One stable logical page entry. Required fields are the frozen contract consumed by design-brief Step 2.

purpose.description: One-line statement of what the page is for.

audience.description: User segment for the page. Open vocabulary; common values include visitor, registered, minor, and guardian. Null means an unresolved conflict that should stop reconstruction and ask the operator.

accessTier.description: Visibility/gate axis, separate from audience. Base values are public and auth; role gates use auth:<role>. Null means an unresolved conflict.

status.description: Lifecycle state of the logical page.

regime.description: Per-page implementation regime from the page's own code evidence: greenfield has no own code/route; brownfield has one.

existence.description: Evidence class for whether the logical page exists in artefacts, code, both, or may only be an alias.

aliases.description: Optional advisory alternate page names or route labels. Aliases are advisory only and must not override the canonical page id.

provenance.description: Per-field provenance keyed by extracted field name, with each value naming the source descriptor that fixed that field.

evidenceHash.description: 0.2 (F1): sha256 of this page's normalized founding evidence (code + doc), e.g. 'sha256:<64 hex>'. Optional in 0.1, REQUIRED in 0.2 (enforced by the top-level allOf). On re-execution the reconstruction compares this per page and re-asks the operator ONLY where it diverges — unifying the old top-level inputsHash staleness signal with the conflict-already-answered fingerprint.

conflicts.description: Unresolved per-field artefact/code conflicts. A pending conflict may force nullable contract fields such as audience or accessTier to null. The per-item shape is version-gated by the top-level allOf (legacy two-slot for 0.1/0.2, witnesses[] for 0.3); this base constraint stays permissive so those branches own the shape.

field.description: Field whose artefact and code evidence disagree.

artefactValue.description: Value found in the non-code artefact source. Deliberately permissive because extracted values can be strings, nulls, arrays, or structured fragments.

codeValue.description: Value found in code evidence. Deliberately permissive because extracted values can be strings, nulls, arrays, or structured fragments.

evidence.description: Source descriptor or short explanation proving the unresolved conflict.

resolution.description: 0.1: a status string (pending|resolved). 0.2: an object recording the operator's arbitration so a confirmed conflict persists as a resolved decision, not a live state — { resolvedBy, resolvedAt, choice }. The oneOf keeps 0.1 catalogs valid while admitting the 0.2 decision-memory shape.

resolvedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the arbitration. Inline (not a cross-file $ref) so the F0 single-file validator stays self-contained.

choice.description: The value the operator chose. Permissive: strings, nulls, or structured fragments.

conflict_0_3.description: 0.3 conflict descriptor (P1 — never choose in silence). A field with two or more discordant witnesses, preserved as a SET so no witness is dropped by format. Replaces the 0.1/0.2 two-slot artefactValue/codeValue form; those slots are rejected here by additionalProperties:false.

witnesses.description: All value-claims for this field, each with its provenance and derived kind. N is uncapped: N=2, N=3, N=7 are all representable (the F2 #2 truncation is no longer expressible).

value.description: The witnessed value. Permissive: strings, nulls, arrays, or structured fragments.

source.description: Provenance of this witness — a source descriptor or path. The producer derives kind by matching this against the page's codeEvidence.path; the integrity of that derivation is enforced by the emit-time validator, not by this schema.

kind.description: Derived-at-source provenance class (P2): code when the witness source matches the page's codeEvidence.path, otherwise artefact. Computed by the producer, never asserted independently.

evidence.description: Source descriptor or short explanation proving the unresolved conflict.

resolution.description: 0.3 keeps the 0.2 decision-memory shape: a pending|resolved status string, or an object { resolvedBy, resolvedAt, choice } recording the operator's arbitration. choice references the winning witness by value+source (not by positional index); the value+source match is enforced by the emit-time validator.

choice.description: The winning witness, identified by value+source. Permissive shape so the validator owns the value+source integrity check.
